session.user:
Run the following CI checks and report results. Do NOT fix any errors - just report.
1. Run: uv run ruff check src/ tests/ packages/ 2. Run: npx prettier --check docs/learned/configuration/issues-repo.md docs/learned/conventions.md docs/learned/erk/branch-naming.md docs/learned/erk/issue-pr-linkage-storage.md docs/learned/glossary.md docs/learned/integrations/issue-pr-closing-integration.md docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md docs/learned/planning/complete-inventory-protocol.md docs/learned/planning/cross-repo-plans.md docs/learned/planning/plan-schema.md docs/learned/planning/pr-discovery.md docs/learned/planning/pr-submission-patterns.md docs/learned/planning/session-deduplication.md docs/learned/reference/changelog-standards.md docs/learned/sessions/discovery-fallback.md 3. Run: make check-exec-reference 4. Run: uv run pytest tests/ -x -q --timeout=10 (just report count)
session.assistant: I'll run these CI checks and report the results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: uv run ruff check src/ tests/ packages/

[ 2%] ...................................F =================================== FAILURES =================================== __________________ test_execute_split_plan_creates_worktrees ___________________
def test_execute_split_plan_creates_worktrees() -> None: """Normal execution creates worktrees."""
# Create fake git ops class FakeGit: def __init__(self): self.created_worktrees = []
def add_worktree(self, repo_root, path, *, branch=None, ref=None, create_branch=True): self.created_worktrees.append((repo_root, path, branch, ref, create_branch))
plan = SplitPlan( stack_branches=["main", "feat-1", "feat-2"], branches_to_split=["feat-1", "feat-2"], existing_worktrees=[], target_paths={ "feat-1": Path("/repo/erks/feat-1"), "feat-2": Path("/repo/erks/feat-2"), }, source_worktree_path=Path("/repo"), repo_root=Path("/repo"), skipped_current=False, skipped_trunk=True,
... [52 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: Now let me run the correct exec-reference-check target:

## CI Check Results
**Summary:**
1. **ruff check**: Passed - All checks passed 2. **prettier check**: Passed - All matched files use Prettier code style 3. **exec-reference-check**: Passed - `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` is up to date 4. **pytest**: Failed - 1 test failure found
**Pytest Failure Details:**
Test: `tests/cli/commands/split/test_plan.py::test_execute_split_plan_creates_worktrees`
Error: `AttributeError: 'FakeGit' object has no attribute 'worktree'`
Location: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/stack/split_old/plan.py:207`
The test's FakeGit object is missing a `worktree` attribute that the code expects. The actual code calls `git_ops.worktree.add_worktree()` but the test mock object only has `add_worktree()` directly.
